meta.name: French Country Lentil Soup
meta.subtitle: Lentilles-style soup with aromatics, herbs, and optional smoked meat.

source.name: ssa.cuisines.french_country
notes: Canonical SSA recipe: French-style lentil soup emphasizing aromatics + herbs, with optional ham hock/bacon for smoky depth.
Designed for batch cooking, leftovers, and freezer storage.

summary: A hearty French country lentil soup: green lentils simmered with onion, carrot, celery, garlic, thyme, and bay, finished with vinegar and herbs for brightness. Optional smoked meat adds depth.

yield.notes: Serves 6 as bowls; 8 as a starter. Thickens as it sits.

notes: Flavor improves after resting 15–30 minutes.
Add vinegar at the end to keep lentils tender and brighten flavor.
Earthy lentils balanced by aromatic mirepoix and bright vinegar finish.
Optional smoked meat adds savory, smoky depth.

optional.label: Immersion blender
optional.notes: Optional: blend a small portion for a creamier body without dairy.

notes: If using butter, contains dairy.
If using bacon/ham hock, contains pork (omit for dietary restrictions).

ingredients.label: Green lentils (French green/lentilles du Puy preferred), rinsed
notes: Green lentils hold their shape better than red lentils.
Avoid pre-salted or seasoned lentil packets for best control.

ingredients.label: Fresh thyme sprigs (or dried thyme)
notes: If using dried thyme: use about 1 tsp.

ingredients.label: Chicken stock (or vegetable stock for vegetarian)
notes: Use low-sodium stock when possible; adjust salt at the end.

ingredients.label: Water (as needed for consistency)
notes: Add more during simmer if the soup thickens too quickly.

ingredients.label: Ham hock or smoked pork shank (optional)
notes: Adds smoky depth and collagen-rich body.
If using, reduce added salt until the end.

ingredients.label: Bacon, diced (optional alternative to ham hock)
notes: Optional: sauté first and use rendered fat in place of some oil.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
notes: Start with less if using ham hock/bacon; adjust at the end.

ingredients.label: Red wine vinegar (or sherry vinegar) for finishing
notes: Add gradually and taste; the goal is brightness, not sourness.

ingredients.label: Dijon mustard (optional swirl for serving)
notes: Classic French touch: a small spoonful in the bowl adds tang and depth.

steps.title: Rinse lentils and prep aromatics
instructions: Rinse lentils under cool water and pick out any small stones or debris.
Dice onion, carrot, and celery. Mince garlic.
checks: Lentils are rinsed and free of debris.
Aromatics are evenly diced for even cooking.

steps.title: Optional: render bacon for a smoky base
instructions: If using bacon, add it to a cold pot and set over medium heat.
Cook, stirring occasionally, until fat renders and bacon is lightly crisp (4–6 minutes).
Remove bacon with a slotted spoon; leave 1–2 tbsp fat in the pot (discard excess or save).
checks: Bacon is lightly crisp and fat is rendered (not burnt).

steps.title: Sauté onion, carrot, and celery
instructions: Add olive oil (and optional butter) to the pot over medium heat (use bacon fat if rendered).
Add onion, carrot, and celery with a pinch of salt.
Cook 8–10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until softened and lightly sweet.
checks: Onion is translucent; carrots/celery are softened but not browned heavily.

steps.title: Bloom garlic and optional tomato paste
instructions: Add garlic and stir 30 seconds until fragrant.
If using tomato paste, stir it in and cook 60 seconds to caramelize slightly.
checks: Tomato paste darkens slightly and smells sweet.

steps.title: Add lentils, herbs, and stock
instructions: Stir in lentils, thyme, and bay leaf.
Add stock and bring to a gentle boil.
If using ham hock, add it now.
checks: Lentils are fully submerged and soup is coming to a gentle boil.

steps.title: Simmer until lentils are tender
instructions: Reduce heat to low and simmer partially covered 35–45 minutes, stirring occasionally.
If soup becomes too thick before lentils are tender, add water in 1/2-cup increments.
Start seasoning with pepper and some salt (use less if ham hock/bacon is used).
checks: Lentils are tender but not mushy (green lentils hold shape).
Soup has a cohesive broth and doesn’t scorch on the bottom.

steps.title: Optional: shred ham hock and return meat
instructions: If using ham hock, remove it to a plate and let cool briefly.
Strip meat from bone, discard skin/fat as desired, chop or shred meat.
Return meat to the pot. (Return reserved bacon if using.)
checks: Meat is bite-size and distributed through the soup.

steps.title: Adjust texture, season fully, and finish with vinegar and herbs
instructions: Remove bay leaf and thyme stems (if using fresh sprigs).
Optional: blend 1–2 cups of soup and return it for a creamier body (or use immersion blender briefly).
Taste and adjust salt and pepper.
Stir in red wine vinegar a teaspoon at a time until the flavor brightens.
Finish with parsley. Serve with optional Dijon in bowls or lemon wedges.
checks: Soup tastes bright and balanced after vinegar addition.
Salt is adjusted last (especially important if meat was used).

notes: Serve with crusty bread and a simple salad.
A small spoon of Dijon in the bowl is a classic French move.
Cool quickly and refrigerate within 2 hours.
Soup thickens; loosen with water/stock when reheating.
Freeze in portions. Thaw overnight in fridge or gently on stovetop.
Add a splash of vinegar or fresh herbs after reheating to refresh flavor.
Reheat gently over medium-low, stirring occasionally to avoid scorching.
Add water/stock as needed to restore brothiness.

core: Cool leftovers promptly and refrigerate within 2 hours.
Bring reheated soup to steaming hot throughout.
If using ham hock, ensure meat is fully heated before serving.

notes: Green lentils keep their shape; if using brown lentils, expect a softer texture.
Avoid adding acidic ingredients (vinegar/lemon) early—acid can slow softening.
For a vegetarian version, use vegetable stock and add a pinch of smoked paprika (optional) for depth.
Use vegetable stock and omit butter (use olive oil only) for vegan.
Optional: add 1/2 tsp smoked paprika or a splash of soy sauce/tamari for umami.
Stir in chopped kale or spinach during the last 5 minutes of simmering.
Brown sliced smoked sausage after sautéing mirepoix; then proceed with lentils and stock.

remixIdeas: Blend thicker leftovers into a lentil spread for toast
Serve over rice with a fried egg
Add extra stock and vegetables for a stretched soup
